title: Allow inspect to work even if tag list blocked
body:
Some registries may choose to block the "list all tags" endpoint for
performance or other reasons. In this case we should still allow an
inspect which will not include the "tag list" in the output.

Signed-off-by: Phil Estes <estesp@gmail.com>

comments.body:
LGTM I guess, the possible improvements mentioned above are not quite blockers from my POV; will defer to @runcom on whether to merge as is.

@estesp FWIW the tests are failing on golint.

Updated to fix the `go lint` issue; also added a specific comment about the Bluemix registry restriction (which will probably go away).

I also agree a typed error would make this side cleaner than matching on the 401 error return in the string. I'm fine if there is a desire to clean up that side first and then add this. Either way is fine with me.
Thanks! I can live with the string matching here, but I’ll defer to @runcom .

Well yeah, it's fine with me even with this for now, I'll work to port this on c/image

LGTM

@estesp If this is still rejected by the IBM Bluemix registry, do you have sample registry response that is returned by an attempt to list tags?

It would be useful to have a permanent test case in https://github.com/containers/image/blob/d2420cbbf4d8c44e827bbeb7ce24abec211fba65/docker/errors_test.go#L18 , to make sure we don’t regress this.

For the record:
- AFAICT, “IBM Bluemix” has become “IBM Cloud”, and the “Bluemix” registry is now (somehow related to?) `icr.io`; e.g. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/Registry?topic=Registry-registry_overview lists `bluemix.net` and `icr.io` host names.
- Randomly looking for a public image hosted on that registry, at least `skopeo list-tags docker://icr.io/codeengine/firstjob` now succeeds.

So I’m assuming that at least the current cloud deployment now allows listing tags, and does not need special handling. (Is that true for all currently deployed versions?)
